preview_text:
The model rates Austria as the favored outcome at 45%, but the gap to Jordan remains material rather than commanding. Jordan's win probability of 24% reflects genuine competitive viability, while the draw emerges as a plausible contingency at 31%.

The spread across the three outcomes is relatively balanced. Austria holds an advantage, yet the 21-percentage-point margin to Jordan and the substantial draw likelihood suggest the match could move in multiple directions. Neither team's path to victory appears heavily favored in isolation.

The sportsbook and Polymarket consensus strongly diverge from the model. Both betting markets assign Austria a 72–74% win probability and Jordan just 9–10%, a gap of roughly 27 percentage points versus the model's 21-point spread. The model sees the draw as twice as likely as the betting consensus does. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the divergence merits attention from users evaluating the fixture.
